meaning: n.欢乐的精神,欢乐的气氛 pl.作乐,乐事
examples: The colourful flags added to the gaiety of the occasion.
Music rang out, adding to the gaiety and life of the market.
She brought him a sense of fun, of gaiety that filled a gap in his life.
Call me a curmudgeon, but I detested those forced moments of gaiety and collegiality.
Thus, learning a new design language, Willis worked his spell, knitting everything together with confidence, subtlety and gaiety.
